target:
WIN-053 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — PATCH the existing string by inserting one new sentence (with leading single space) immediately AFTER the verbatim substring "only a relabelling of it)." and BEFORE the "

" that introduces the "A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form." paragraph. No other paragraph, sentence, field, or TLDR is altered. The insertion adds a third-horn closure (the metaphysical/aetheric-retreat defense) to the forced disjunction introduced by EXP-194, so paragraph 2 now concludes with three horns instead of two.
source_issue: ISS-1302 (WIN-053.c7 hole 3 — FORCED DISJUNCTION MISSES THE METAPHYSICAL RETREAT)
patch_instructions:
In data/wins.json, locate the WIN with id='053'. In its detail_evidence field, find the unique substring "only a relabelling of it).

" (occurrence count = 1 as of 2026-04-22, verified against current wins.json). Replace that substring with "only a relabelling of it). " + replacement_sentence + "

". Do not modify any other paragraph. Do not modify detail_verdict_text, detail_claim, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, or tldr_verdict. TLDRs do not surface the metaphysical-retreat defense and require no change. Verify after patch that WIN-053 detail_evidence still parses cleanly in the dashboard / index renderer (the new sentence contains one anchor following the existing #p6-61-1 convention used in tldr_verdict).

anchor.after: A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.

replacement_sentence: A third retreat — claiming the toroidal flow is aetheric rather than Maxwellian, so ∇·B = 0 does not apply and flux conservation is simply inapplicable — is the defender's strongest remaining move, but it abandons the very claim WIN-053 rests on. Once interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry: B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit to observed pole magnitudes (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1), the dome's 20% RMS "improvement" is no longer a derivation at all but a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no predictive work.
holes_addressed: WIN-053.c7 H3 (moderate, FORCED DISJUNCTION MISSES THE METAPHYSICAL RETREAT): EXP-194 closed the two advocate-mode horns (surface-prediction vs crustal-bridge-into-geodynamo) but left the defender's strongest remaining escape — the metaphysical/aetheric retreat — unrebutted on-page. The new sentence puts the third horn in front of the reader, credits it as a legitimate move in physics (it is the defender's strongest remaining escape), and closes it by showing that waiving interior flux conservation unmoors A, C, and λ from any derivation in dome geometry — collapsing B(r) into a parameterless surface fit and folding the claim into the §6.1 'observed values relabeled' / 'zero fitted parameters' critique already in hand.
new_evidence_added: Explicit articulation of the metaphysical/aetheric retreat as the defender's strongest remaining move (previously only acknowledged in EXP-194's anticipated_objections, never surfaced in reader-facing prose).

Link from the retreat branch to §6.1 ('Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters') — the existing cross-reference anchor used by tldr_verdict. The detail_evidence paragraph 2 thereby gets a cross-ref that matches the style already used by paragraph 3 and the TLDR, closing a minor consistency gap the c7 review also flagged (H6).

Framing that the aetheric retreat demotes the 20% RMS improvement from 'derivation' to 'curve fit' — a single-clause bite that links horn three back to the post-hoc retrodiction framing the verdict already carries.

anticipated_objections.objection: The defender could accept the aetheric framing AND claim the toroidal geometry still 'predicts' the dipolar form via aether-to-matter coupling that happens to produce Maxwellian surface fields matching magnetometer readings.
anticipated_objections.response: That move requires specifying the coupling — a new physical mechanism with its own parameters — which the dome does not provide. Without a specified coupling law, the claim that 'aether flow in this geometry produces this particular B(r)' is unfalsifiable. With a specified coupling law, we would check whether the coupling introduces additional fitted parameters (almost certainly yes), and the §6.1 hidden-parameters critique widens. Either way WIN-053's claimed derivation from geometry is not restored.

anticipated_objections.objection: The defender could argue that 'aetheric field' is just a label, and the dome's B(r) is a Maxwellian field whose source is simply described in aetheric-flow language — so ∇·B = 0 applies, flux conservation applies, and we're back to horn one without any new concession.
anticipated_objections.response: This is not horn three at all — it is horn one under a cosmetic relabel. If the dome accepts Maxwell, flux conservation in the dome's own ring-magnet circuit forces B_south ≈ 39 nT against the fitted 64,852 nT — the 1,660:1 contradiction stated in paragraph 1 of detail_evidence. The defender cannot invoke horn three rhetorically ('our flow is aetheric') while quietly retaining Maxwell's equations for the quantitative physics; the choice has consequences.

anticipated_objections.objection: The closure language 'observed values relabeled' is loaded — the defender could reject it as not a neutral description.
anticipated_objections.response: The phrase is already in WIN-053's tldr_verdict cross-referencing §6.1 and in Section 6.4's title ('Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions'). The third-horn sentence uses the same phrase consistently with the rest of the review. The appendix §6.1 documents why the relabeling label applies: A, C, and λ match observed pole strengths and the H(r) scale length, with no derivation from dome parameters. The label is earned, not imported.

kernel_of_truth: The metaphysical retreat IS a legitimate move in physics. Whole research programmes (modified gravity, dark sector models, emergent-spacetime approaches) proceed by saying 'the standard equations don't apply in regime X, here is the replacement theory.' Acknowledging this move respectfully is important — waving it away as obviously invalid would make our review sound dogmatic. But the dome does not provide a replacement theory. It proposes a single equation B(r) in SI nanotesla units, fits it to observed pole magnitudes, and calls the fit a 'prediction' of toroidal architecture. The retreat asserts that the interior flow obeys different physics than Maxwell without specifying what those physics are; that is not a parallel to modified gravity (which supplies field equations) but a rhetorical escape hatch. Once we credit the move and examine its consequences, we see it destroys the claim WIN-053 is trying to make — the toroidal geometry stops doing predictive work and B(r) becomes a surface fit. Stating this plainly, rather than dismissing the retreat, is both more intellectually honest and more persuasive.
cross_references: WIN-053 paragraph 1 (1,600:1 ratio) — horn one's flux-conservation bite, referenced in anticipated_objection #2.
WIN-053 paragraph 2 (EXP-194 insertion) — horns one and two; the new sentence appends horn three to this paragraph.
WIN-053 paragraph 3 (coefficient circularity) — reinforces horn three's fitted-parameter conclusion.

Section 6.1 'Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters' — the §6.1 anchor used here matches the anchor already used in tldr_verdict, creating consistent cross-referencing across WIN-053's surface forms.

EXP-194 anticipated_objections entry #1 — identified the metaphysical retreat as a possible defender move, with the response 'concedes the central WIN-053 claim.' The new sentence surfaces that private analysis to the reader.
unaddressed_holes_note: EXP-219 is scoped narrowly to ISS-1302 (c7 H3) only. Other WIN-053.c7 holes remain separately tracked: H1 (major, internal inconsistency between paragraph 2's geographic-south-pole comparator and paragraph 3's Magnetic-Pole comparator) and H2 (moderate, 'explicitly INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7' overclaim — note: current detail_evidence wording reads 'the surface-observatory sources the dome's own home page highlights (INTERMAGNET ground stations and CHAOS-7, which the dome mischaracterizes as "paleomagnetic" but which is actually a present-day satellite-and-observatory composite)', so this may already be resolved by a prior decider patch). H4/H5 (minor, crustal-conduction label + 'thousands of km of rock') remain open. ISS-1243/1244/1245/1246 (c5 holdovers) also remain open per c7 confirmation. None of these are within scope for ISS-1302.
notes_for_decider:
Single-sentence insertion. Patch is a plain string replace: find "only a relabelling of it).

" (exactly one occurrence), replace with "only a relabelling of it). " + replacement_sentence + "

". No JSON schema fields other than detail_evidence are touched. TLDRs (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) are intentionally not amended — both already foreground the flux-conservation argument and the §6.1 cross-reference; TLDR review standard (from analyst.md) is layman readability, not adding every detail the expanded argument now carries. After integration, please verify (a) the paragraph reads cleanly in the dashboard / index renderer, (b) the onclick='showTab(\'predictions\')' syntax survives JSON round-tripping intact, (c) running tests produce no new failures. The new sentence reuses the '#p6-61-1' anchor convention already present in tldr_verdict — same anchor, same onclick handler.
deeper_analysis:
The c7 review flagged three moderate-severity holes in EXP-194. ISS-1302 targets H3 specifically: the forced disjunction as written leaves the defender's strongest escape (the metaphysical/aetheric retreat) unrebutted in reader-facing prose. EXP-194's own anticipated_objections considered this move privately and concluded it 'concedes the central WIN-053 claim,' but the conclusion never made it onto the page. A sophisticated defender reading paragraph 2 today sees two horns, notes that neither applies if the toroidal flow is 'aetheric' rather than Maxwellian, and walks away. EXP-219 fixes that. The third-horn sentence credits the retreat respectfully (it is the defender's strongest remaining move), then shows its cost: waiving interior flux conservation unmoors A, C, and λ from toroidal geometry, B(r) becomes a parameterless surface fit, and the 20% RMS 'improvement' becomes a curve fit rather than a derivation. The claim that 'toroidal architecture predicts the observed dipole' — the WIN's headline — is sacrificed.

I considered adding a unit-calibration bite (nanotesla is defined by the Lorentz force F = q·v×B, so the dome cannot coherently report B(r) in Maxwellian units while disclaiming Maxwell). This argument has real force but it opens a side-quest around aether-to-matter coupling that a defender could extend indefinitely. The §6.1 fitted-parameter closure is more durable, cross-references infrastructure we already have, and keeps the third-horn sentence tight. The unit-calibration point is logged in anticipated_objections.

Other side of the argument — a few places where the defender might push back:
  (1) 'You are demanding we specify new physics whenever we say Maxwell doesn't apply.' — yes, that is the standard; every modification-of-standard-physics programme (MOND, bimetric gravity, etc.) supplies field equations. The dome supplies an equation but declares its ancestor geometry metaphysical; that is asymmetric in a way that damages the claim.
  (2) 'You are begging the question by calling B(r) a surface fit.' — we are not: the §6.1 section already shows B(r) has no derivation from dome parameters, and the c7 dome_code_analysis confirms no monitor.py domain or code-side derivation. The label is earned.
  (3) 'Your phrase "strongest remaining move" over-credits the retreat.' — better to over-credit than under-credit. Saying the move is strong foregrounds the closure; a sneering dismissal would make the closure feel unearned. This is the 'find the kernel of truth' discipline from analyst.md.

The sentence uses the same §6.1 anchor already present in tldr_verdict (#p6-61-1 with showTab('predictions')) for style consistency. It fits the existing detail_evidence pattern of internal cross-references.
tldr_change: None. The TLDRs (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) already foreground the flux-conservation argument and the §6.1 cross-reference; neither currently invokes the metaphysical retreat, and adding a third-horn reference to the TLDR would bloat a text whose purpose is layman readability. Per analyst.md TLDR review standard: 'Fix factual errors. Do not split hairs on nuance — the expanded detail handles that.' The detail_evidence handles it.
